copyright.declaration.english.message: All photography works on this site are protected under the Copyright Law of the PRC. Unauthorized use is strictly prohibited.

about.profile.bio.message: BJTU Incoming PhD Student, Computer Science ➡️ Cybersecurity. I study software supply chain vulnerabilities and constitutional AI in multicultural contexts. I care deeply about learning science and cognitive security.

about.recently.agent.message: Software Supply Chain Vulnerability Detection Agent

about.quote.ml.message: "sukima-ml stands for Sukima Moonlight, but Machine Learning? I can do that too 😉"

about.quote.vision.message: Bringing more cultural depth to Touhou products. School is draining; in my next life, I'd like to be a lovable, wealthy genius girl.

about.solo.notice.message: * Currently, this circle is just me working solo, but I'm excited about what we'll create together!

contact.description.message: Connect with Sukima Moonlight and share your creativity

contact.quote.message: "We are always looking for new ideas and collaborations. Whether you are an artist, a developer, or just a fan of Touhou Project, we'd love to hear from you."

contact.feedback.desc.message: Feel free to share your ideas, product suggestions, or anything interesting in the comments below!

contact.feedback.tip.message: 💡 Tip: Supports Markdown; you can use image hosting links to insert images

gallery.description.message: Explore Touhou Project characters reimagined through classic masterpieces and Giclée art prints.

giclee.description.message: More than just merch; it's a 'true trace' leading to Gensokyo

giclee.header.part2.message: this is the 'true trace' leading to

giclee.intro.p1.line1.message: We've always been thinking: when those magnificent world-famous paintings are dipped into the vat of fantasy and return to reality, how should they arrive by our side?

giclee.intro.p1.line2.message: As common, glare-heavy posters? Or rough-surfaced plastic sheets that curl and fade after hanging for a while?

giclee.intro.vision.message: The girls of Gensokyo deserve the highest level of treatment.

giclee.intro.p2.message: To perfectly replicate the gorgeous world under the artist's brush, we follow {standard} to create all our 'Giclée' products.

giclee.intro.p3.message: This is not just a picture, but a piece of physical collection that can be passed down.

giclee.sec1.title.message: 🎨 Only 12 Colors can capture the depth of the 'Gap'

giclee.sec1.desc1.message: Ordinary printing only has 4 CMYK colors, failing to match {purple} or restore {red}.

giclee.sec1.purple.message: the deep purple in Yakumo Yukari's gaps

giclee.sec1.red.message: the vibrant red of the Hakurei Shrine Torii

giclee.sec1.desc2.message: So, we deployed Epson's flagship beast — {model}.

giclee.sec1.image.caption.message: Epson SureColor P9580 Flagship Large Format Printer

giclee.sec1.item1.desc.message: It features {colors} inks that ordinary printers lack. Those brilliant lights and shadows can only be accurately restored by it.

giclee.sec1.item2.desc.message: With four different black inks, the blacks are not flat and dead, but layered and textured like the night sky.

giclee.sec1.item3.desc.message: With 2400 dpi resolution and 3.5 pl micro-droplets, the eyelashes and strands of hair remain so clear that no visible dot pattern appears to the naked eye.

giclee.sec2.desc1.message: If ink is the soul, then paper is the body. We refused cheap photo paper and chose Hahnemühle from Germany — a legendary brand that has been making paper since 1584.

giclee.sec2.purpleNote.message: (Yukari’s print is special; because of its large dark areas, it uses a more expensive barium sulfate cotton paper.)

giclee.sec2.item1.desc.message: Run a fingertip over it and you can feel the warm cotton texture of old books and watercolor paper, nothing like ordinary pulp paper.

giclee.sec2.item2.desc.message: It feels substantial in the hand: firm, thick, and nothing like a limp ordinary poster.

giclee.sec2.item3.desc.message: {focus} It is completely non-reflective. No matter how your room light hits it, the image stays viewable from every angle without harsh glare.

giclee.sec2.detailHint.message: To make the difference easier to see, we photographed actual finished details:

giclee.sec3.desc.message: A good print deserves a good frame. We are almost obsessive about protecting the work:

giclee.sec3.item1.desc.message: Clearer, lighter, and safer than glass.

giclee.sec3.item2.desc.message: Moisture-resistant and warp-resistant, giving the artwork a firm structure.

giclee.sec3.item3.desc.message: Keeps the print from yellowing due to contact with acidic materials.

giclee.sec4.desc1.part1.message: Ordinary prints fade after a year in sunlight.

giclee.sec4.desc1.part2.message: But thanks to the combination of {paper} and {ink}, this work reaches a {standard}.

giclee.sec4.desc2.message: It can accompany you through high school, university, and even into adult life. When you look back, the girls on the wall will still feel as vivid as the first day.

giclee.conclusion.message: We hope that what we sell is not just a print, but a piece of love that can transcend time and is worth cherishing.

buy.description.message: Purchase instructions and payment methods for Sukima Moonlight products.

buy.contact.emailFirst.message: Email is recommended. You can use any mailbox, including QQ Mail; confirmations and tracking numbers will be sent by reply email.

buy.contact.afterPayment.message: After sending the purchase email, please join the circle QQ group. I will confirm the order by email and provide shipping information. If anything goes wrong, contact me by email or private message in the group.

buy.template.contactLine.message: Name, phone number, and shipping address

buy.template.note.payment.message: * Please attach a payment screenshot. The screenshot must clearly show the merchant order number or transaction/order ID so the organizer can verify the payment.

buy.template.note.dm.message: * If you choose to buy by private message, please provide the same information above.

gallery.art.001.title.message: The 17-Year-Old Girl with a Pearl Earring

gallery.art.001.subtitle.message: The Girl with a Pearl Earring (Age 17)

gallery.art.004.desc.message: Why is Sanae sitting on the swing, stretching out white stockings with such theatrical charm? Why is Aya hiding in the lower-left corner to catch the decisive moment? Fragonard’s The Swing has slipped into Youkai Mountain.

gallery.art.001.desc.message: Oh my, traveler from the outside world, lost all the way here. A pleasure. This portrait caught a true glimpse of me outside the gaps. If you hang it on the wall of your heart and look at it often, perhaps that too is a kind of offering to my existence.

gallery.art.003.desc.message: A slice of everyday life at Eientei. Compared with the solemn Spanish court of the original painting, the air here feels ready to erupt into a spell-card incident at any moment. Notice the figure secretly photographing from the doorway.

gallery.art.002.desc.message: Is this the weight of knowledge? To reach truth, someone must become the foundation, such as the poor little devil trembling beneath Lady Patchouli’s feet right now.

gallery.art.004.introOriginal.message: Fragonard’s Rococo masterpiece shows a young woman sweeping upward on a swing while a hidden lover watches below, capturing the frivolous pleasure of 18th-century French court life.

gallery.art.001.introOriginal.message: Vermeer’s celebrated portrait turns a half-spoken glance, luminous lips, and the glow of a pearl earring into one of art history’s most mysterious moments, often called the Mona Lisa of the North.

gallery.art.003.introOriginal.message: Velázquez’s masterpiece uses complex perspective, mirrored reflections, and the painter’s own presence to blur reality and illusion, becoming one of art history’s great riddles about seeing.

gallery.art.002.introOriginal.message: Carl Spitzweg’s classic figure shows a scholar on a ladder, completely absorbed in books and oblivious to reality, embodying the Biedermeier era’s eccentric hunger for knowledge.

gallery.art.004.introTouhou.message: Move the scene to Youkai Mountain and the girl on the swing becomes Sanae, while the hidden observer catching the decisive moment is no aristocratic lover but Aya the reporter. Perhaps this is Gensokyo’s version of tabloid photography.

gallery.art.001.introTouhou.message: In Gensokyo, the sitter becomes this eternally 17-year-old girl. Compared with the pearl’s gentle glow, Yukari’s all-seeing gaze and the cracked, timeworn texture feel even more like a charm that crosses boundaries.

gallery.art.003.introTouhou.message: In the Eientei version, the proud Princess Kaguya becomes the visual center. Eirin serves tea with ceremony, while Mokou seems forced into public relations. Less court etiquette, more grand cosplay born from immortal boredom.

gallery.art.002.introTouhou.message: The witch of the Scarlet Devil Mansion perfectly recreates that devotion to books. But the ladder has become a very unfortunate familiar. For asthmatic Patchouli, reading ten thousand volumes without moving may be the ultimate magic.
